name: Week 10 Pull — Form-First Return (Spotlight: Pull-up + DB Row)
description: Form-first cycle, weights pulled back ~10-15% from W8 highs. **Spotlight lifts:** pull-up (drill the active-hang start + elbows-down-to-ribs cue) and DB row (drill the chest-supported torso lock + elbow-to-hip line). Everything else holds. Pyramid sets stay (DB row), but the heaviest set comes off — it's about clean reps, not chasing 85.

dayOverview.theme: Form-first return from W9 deload. Pull-up pattern + row pattern get drilled. Modern 2026 cues over chasing reps.

spotlights.why: W7 hit 32 with mixed grips, but cumulative fatigue made later sets sloppy. W10 lock in the active-hang start position + elbows-down-to-ribs pull pattern so every rep is structurally honest, not just a chin-over.
W7 hit 80x10 clean on bench. Drill the chest-supported torso lock (no rotation cheating) + elbow-to-hip pull line. The 80 stays as top set — no 85 push this week, the cue is the progression.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W10 pull-up + DB row · W11 chest-supported row + lat pulldown · W12 face pull + reverse fly · W13 preacher curl + high cable curl + cable crunch. ~4 weeks per cycle, then refresh.
watchpoints: Grip fatigue limiting lat pulldown more than back strength (W3 onward) — straps are an option if it persists
Wider grip on lat pulldown reduces bicep involvement — keep flexible, wide on heavy pull-up days
Reverse fly: seated bent-over is the default form (W3 discovery), not standing

exercises.notes: Scap activation. Squeeze the shoulder blades together at the back, soft elbow.
Spine warm — slow, full ROM each direction.
**Active hang, not collapsed** — engage the lats, pull the shoulders down away from the ears even at full extension. This is the position the pull-up starts from.

**LOAD:** Bodyweight. Vary grips across sets — climber handles, wide, normal.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every set:**
- **Start from an ACTIVE hang** — at full extension, lats engaged, shoulders pulled DOWN away from ears. Not a dead/collapsed hang. The active hang IS the start position.
- **Pull elbows DOWN to the ribs**, NOT chin to bar. The chin clearing is a marker; the work is the lat squeeze.
- **Chest leads to bar** at the top — drive the chest forward, mid-back squeezed, no neck craning.
- **Eyes ahead, head neutral** — no chin tucking, no head pumping. The neck is along for the ride.
- **Slow eccentric**, ~2-3 seconds down, return to active hang (not collapsed).
- **No kipping.** If you can't get a clean rep, the set ends — better to do 6 strict than 8 with kip.

**Reflection:** which grip felt the most honest today? That's a clue about which lat angle is strongest right now.

**LOAD:** 12.5/side (back from W8's 15/side). Sharp cues only — spotlight is W13.
- **Armpit pressed firmly into the pad** — not just resting. Back of upper arm flat against the pad.
- Full stretch at the bottom — don't lose tension by curling short
- Curl toward face, not chest
- 3s eccentric — fight the long-head stretch on the way down

**Swap:** if preacher is taken, incline curl at 25 (W7+ backup, proven solid — `incline-curl`, NOT concentration curl; user has never done concentration variation).

**LOAD:** Pyramid 60x10, 70x10, 80x8 (no 85 this week — cleanest 80 you can do is the goal).

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every set:**
- **Chest-supported on the incline bench** — chest fully on the bench, no arching, head neutral. The bench eliminates torque cheating.
- **Pull elbow toward HIP, not toward armpit** — toward armpit = rear delt; toward hip = lat.
- **Don't rotate the torso** — both shoulder blades stay parallel to the floor at all times. Rotation = unilateral lat torque, fakes the weight.
- **Slow 2s descent**, brief 1s squeeze at the top
- **Don't slam the DB at the bottom** — control the stretch, that's where the lat lengthens
- Head stays neutral throughout — no looking up to help heave

**Reflection:** does the elbow-to-hip line feel different from rowing-toward-armpit? You should feel mid-back instead of rear delt.

sets.name: Station 2: Chest Supported Row + Lat Pulldown

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 35lb (back from W8's 40-45). Hold week — spotlight is W11.
- Chest stays glued to the bench — if it lifts off, the weight is too heavy
- Same elbow-to-hip cue as DB row
- 1s squeeze at top, 2s eccentric

**LOAD:** 145 strict (back from W8's 160). Hold week.
- **Wide grip near the bend** — reduces bicep involvement, isolates lats
- Slight lean back ~15°, NO further (more = becomes a row)
- **Pull bar to upper chest** — never behind the neck (shoulder grinder)
- Elbows down and back, "lats to back pockets"
- 1s pause at the bottom, 2s return to full overhead stretch

**LOAD:** 45 (back from W8's 50). Hold week — slow tempo is the stimulus.
- Rope at upper-chest / face height, step back to set tension
- **Pull rope to face, ELBOWS HIGH (above shoulders)**
- **External rotate at the top** — knuckles point UP, like a double-biceps pose
- 1s pause at peak contraction
- Slow controlled return

**LOAD:** 22.5 (back from W8's 25). Hold week.
- **Seated bent-over** — chest toward thighs (the W3 discovery, default form)
- Soft elbow ~10-15°, fixed throughout
- Lead with elbow, hand passive
- Wings out and slightly UP to shoulder height
- Slow controlled both directions, no swing

sets.name: Finisher: High Cable Curl + Cable Crunch

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 50 (back from W8's 60). 2 sets only.
- Stand between cables, arms out at shoulder level
- **Upper arms parallel to floor, fixed** — if elbows drop, becomes a regular curl
- Curl hand to ear, peak squeeze
- Slow eccentric, no bounce at start

**LOAD:** 100 (back from W8's 110-120). 2 sets only.
- Kneel under high cable, rope behind head, **HIPS LOCKED**
- **Crunch from the abs only** — spine flexes, hips don't move. Hips moving = hip flexor work, not abs.
- Chest toward thighs, hold the crunch 1 second
- Slow eccentric

tips: **FORM-FIRST WEEK** — every weight is intentionally below W8. The progression IS the form, not the load.
**Spotlight lifts** are pull-up + DB row — drill those cues every set. Other lifts get sharp/short cues only.
**Rotation plan:** W11 chest-supported row + lat pulldown · W12 face pull + reverse fly · W13 preacher curl + finisher work.
Pull-up: clean strict reps only. If a rep can't be clean, the set ends. Better 6 strict than 8 with kip.
DB row: pyramid stays (60/70/80) but no 85 push this week. The cleanest 80 you've ever done is the win.
Cable row is permanently gone. Chest supported row is the replacement (W7 lock-in).
Rest: 2.5 min pull-ups, 2 min stations 1-2, 90s station 3, 60s finisher.
formPhilosophy: Form-first base-building. Modern 2026 cues, not 2010 bro-science. Active hangs > dead hangs. Elbow-to-hip > elbow-to-armpit. ROM, tempo, and joint stacking are the progressions. Building decades, not weeks.
